2605.30344.title: Tiny but Trusted: Efficient Vision-Language Reasoning for Time-Series Anomaly Detection
2605.30344.paper_summary: Recent advances in Vision-Language Models (VLMs) have achieved impressive performance across many tasks, yet prior studies report unsatisfactory performance when applying large language or multimodal models to finding abnormal patterns in sequential data. Public anomaly detection benchmarks typically provide interval annotations but not natural-language rationales, making it difficult to fine-tune VLMs to produce grounded, interpretable decisions. To address this gap, we construct VisAnomBench, a curated benchmark built from public time-series datasets and augmented with high-quality anomaly explanations selected from multiple large VLMs using fine-grained, task-specific rewards. Through fine-tuning on this benchmark, we develop VisAnomReasoner, a parameter-efficient VLM for time-series anomaly detection. Experimental results on VisAnomBench show that VisAnomReasoner achieves more accurate anomaly localization and consistently outperforms all baselines, with improvements of at least 21.23 and 23.87 percentage points in precision and F1, respectively. Additional experiments on the TSB-AD-U benchmark demonstrate strong cross-benchmark generalization, with VisAnomReasoner improving precision and F1 by 9.57 and 13.39 percentage points, respectively.
2605.30344.paper_summary_zh: 最近在視覺-語言模型（VLMs）方面的進展在許多任務中取得了令人印象深刻的表現，然而先前的研究報告指出，當將大型語言或多模態模型應用於尋找序列數據中的異常模式時，表現不盡理想。公共異常檢測基準通常提供區間標註，但不提供自然語言的解釋，這使得微調VLMs以產生有根據且可解釋的決策變得困難。為了解決這一問題，我們構建了VisAnomBench，這是一個基於公共時間序列數據集的精心策劃的基準，並通過使用細緻的、特定任務的獎勵從多個大型VLMs中選擇高質量的異常解釋進行增強。通過在這個基準上進行微調，我們開發了VisAnomReasoner，這是一個參數高效的VLM，用於時間序列異常檢測。在VisAnomBench上的實驗結果顯示，VisAnomReasoner在異常定位方面達到了更高的準確性，並且始終超越所有基準，精度和F1的改進分別達到至少21.23和23.87個百分點。在TSB-AD-U基準上的額外實驗顯示出強大的跨基準泛化能力，VisAnomReasoner的精度和F1分別提高了9.57和13.39個百分點。
2605.30344.author: Xiaona Zhou et.al.
2605.30344.authors: Xiaona Zhou, Muntasir Wahed, Tianjiao Yu, Constantin Brif, Ismini Lourentzou
